name: Record Selection
description: Identifies one or more records within a partition that satisfy a selection rule and flags them. In a graph context, downstream transformations resolve values by traversing to the flagged record(s) — no value copying is required. Covers baseline selection (ABLFL), analysis record flags (ANL01FL), worst/best post-baseline selection, and any other record-level flagging pattern.

formula.notes: The selection rule is applied within each partition. Cardinality configuration controls whether one or multiple records can be flagged per partition. Scope configuration controls which records are eligible for selection.

configurations.name: selection_rule
configurations.description: Rule for selecting record(s) within the partition

configurations.name: scope
configurations.description: Which records are eligible for selection

configurations.name: cardinality
configurations.description: Whether the rule selects one record or multiple records per partition

configurations.name: reference_event
configurations.description: The event that defines scope boundaries (e.g. first dose date, randomisation date)

output_classes.description: Indicator marking selected record(s) ('Y' on selected, blank otherwise). In ADaM context: ABLFL for baseline, ANL01FL for analysis records, etc.

assumptions: At least one eligible record exists within the partition for the given scope
Time ordering of records within partition is unambiguous

input_roles.name: value
input_roles.description: The observed values to evaluate for selection

input_roles.name: partition
input_roles.description: Variables defining the selection scope (e.g. subject, parameter)

input_roles.name: time_order
input_roles.description: Variable defining temporal ordering within partition (e.g. visit number, analysis date)
